title: AI foundations capstone

publication.changeSummary: Source-linked AI Foundations draft for AI foundations capstone; all claims, examples, metrics, code, safety boundaries, and answer oracles require independent human review. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

canonicalExplanation.summary: An AI foundations capstone implements a modest bounded system, compares a simple baseline, preserves data/source provenance, evaluates normal and stress cases, documents limitations and risks, and supports reproducible review.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A local search or retrieval prototype includes a deterministic baseline, versioned corpus, held-out queries, failure taxonomy, risk register, model card, and exact reproduction commands. Your goal is to deliver and defend a reproducible project, evaluation report, model card and risk register without overstating capability. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Evaluation and responsible AI; Language, perception and embodied applications; Model card and human oversight; Govern, map, measure and manage. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Bounded operational model
sections.body: An AI foundations capstone implements a modest bounded system, compares a simple baseline, preserves data/source provenance, evaluates normal and stress cases, documents limitations and risks, and supports reproducible review. Any capability or quality claim remains limited to an explicit task, population, conditions, baseline, and recorded evidence.

sections.heading: Failure boundary and non-claim
sections.body: A demo is not deployment evidence, external model output is nondeterministic, test data may be small, RAG and agents add separate failure surfaces, automation may not be justified, and unfinished risks must remain visible. The learner must reject this overclaim: A polished AI demo with a strong average score is a complete capstone and can be described as production-ready.

includes: Implement a modest symbolic, learned or retrieval-grounded system and evaluate it against baseline, stress and risk criteria.
Deliver and defend a reproducible project, evaluation report, model card and risk register without overstating capability.
excludes: A demo is not deployment evidence, external model output is nondeterministic, test data may be small, RAG and agents add separate failure surfaces, automation may not be justified, and unfinished risks must remain visible.

outcomes: Deliver and defend a reproducible project, evaluation report, model card and risk register without overstating capability.

examples.title: AI foundations capstone: bounded direct case
examples.situation: A local search or retrieval prototype includes a deterministic baseline, versioned corpus, held-out queries, failure taxonomy, risk register, model card, and exact reproduction commands.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the exact data, state, metric, or tool behavior: A local search or retrieval prototype includes a deterministic baseline, versioned corpus, held-out queries, failure taxonomy, risk register, model card, and exact reproduction commands.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.

Step 3 — Verify the claim boundary with: Deliver bounded implementation plus baseline, reproducibility, provenance, evaluation slices, stress failures, oversight, risk controls, and claims limited to the recorded evidence.
examples.result: This example supports only the atomic outcome because Deliver bounded implementation plus baseline, reproducibility, provenance, evaluation slices, stress failures, oversight, risk controls, and claims limited to the recorded evidence.

examples.title: AI foundations capstone: stress and transfer case
examples.situation: The capstone reports that evidence is insufficient for autonomous action and keeps a human confirmation step rather than overstating completion.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name the changed population, input, assumption, or failure: The capstone reports that evidence is insufficient for autonomous action and keeps a human confirmation step rather than overstating completion.

Step 2 — Keep the non-claim visible: A demo is not deployment evidence, external model output is nondeterministic, test data may be small, RAG and agents add separate failure surfaces, automation may not be justified, and unfinished risks must remain visible.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting overclaim: A polished AI demo with a strong average score is a complete capstone and can be described as production-ready.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Deliver and defend a reproducible project, evaluation report, model card and risk register without overstating capability.

limitations: documentation and a polished demo are bounded evidence, not production authorization or proof of safe capability

recap.summary: You should now be able to deliver and defend a reproducible project, evaluation report, model card and risk register without overstating capability. Keep this boundary in view: A demo is not deployment evidence, external model output is nondeterministic, test data may be small, RAG and agents add separate failure surfaces, automation may not be justified, and unfinished risks must remain visible.
